title: Aureline queue order-reason and hidden-scope record
description: Boundary schema for the queue-order-reason record minted by `crates/aureline-shell/src/dashboard_truth/model.rs`. One record is rendered identically across the review inbox, incident queue, support queue, and admin queue on desktop shell, CLI / headless inspect, diagnostics, and support exports. It answers two operator questions for a queue: why is each visible row sorted where it is (per-row order_reason), and what is hidden from this view by which narrowing reason (hidden_scope counters). Every visible row carries a canonical open_details_ref and every hidden bucket carries a canonical reveal_ref of the form aureline://<class>/<id> so the chrome routes to the durable object behind the row, never to a generic landing page. Raw endpoint URLs, hostnames, credentials, raw payloads, raw operator identity strings, and absolute paths never cross this boundary.

queue_order_reason_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the queue-order-reason payload. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short user-facing sentence safe to render across shell, CLI/headless inspect, diagnostics, and support exports. Never carries raw payloads.

canonical_object_ref.description: Canonical durable-object ref of the form aureline://<class>/<id>. The <class> segment MUST be a specific object class, never a generic landing page (home, dashboard, landing, index, overview, start, root); the builder rejects those. The chrome routes open-details / reveal affordances here.

surface_token.description: Closed dashboard/queue surface vocabulary. A queue-order-reason record is only minted for queue surfaces (review_inbox, incident_queue, support_queue, admin_queue).

order_reason_token.description: Closed order-reason vocabulary. Surfaces MUST quote a token from this set when explaining why a row is sorted where it is.

narrowing_reason_token.description: Closed narrowing-reason vocabulary. policy_scope and offline_partial_list represent rows whose existence is unknown to the operator (incomplete knowledge), not deliberately filtered.

row_id.description: Row id; matches a dashboard_freshness_card card_id on the same view.

order_rank.description: 1-based render position. The first visible row is rank 1.

hidden_count.description: How many rows are hidden for this reason. A bucket with zero hidden rows is not emitted.

incomplete_knowledge.description: True when the hidden rows are unknown to the operator (policy_scope or offline_partial_list) rather than deliberately filtered.

order_reasons_present.description: Sorted, de-duplicated set of order reasons present across the visible rows.

narrowing_present.description: True when hidden_total > 0 — the operator must know the list is narrowed.

incomplete_knowledge_present.description: True when any hidden bucket has incomplete_knowledge set.
